mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, prompting equity market declines over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction driven by cost pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, with a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced corporate output, driving negative short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >70% of cases during tightening cycles, or the signal shows no statistical edge with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP and housing starts reflects robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of periods with expanding GDPC1 and HOUST, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240, inflation expectations become entrenched, forcing aggressive monetary policy responses that compress equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 240, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns or shows a Rank IC below 0.02 during expansion phases, or payrolls rise while unemployment falls without equity appreciation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during periods of elevated inflation signal consumer pullback and reduced corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines during high inflation are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to produce a statistically significant negative IC.
